Witnesses

Where Did the Accident Occur?
📍
1st Floor — Workers were observed inside the restroom area performing ceiling wire installation in accordance with ongoing project activities at the NCISD New Caney High School jobsite.
How Did the Accident Occur?

While performing overhead ceiling wire installation, the worker was applying downward force to the wire in order to straighten it for placement. The wire unexpectedly snapped, and the sudden release of tension caused the worker's left hand to forcefully descend and strike the guardrail of the aerial lift (scissor lift) being utilized at the time — resulting in a direct impact to the left index finger.

Root Cause Analysis
Was the accident caused by unsafe employee behavior?
No. The worker was properly performing overhead ceiling wire installation and was straightening the wire for placement when it unexpectedly ripped apart and snapped. This was not the result of any unsafe act or disregard for established safety procedures.
Was the accident caused by an equipment defect or failure?
No. The worker was utilizing appropriate PPE including safety gloves and was properly tied off with fall protection while operating the aerial lift (scissor lift). No equipment deficiencies were identified as contributing factors.
Corrective / Preventive Action
Inspect all materials prior to installation. Each wire must be verified to be stable, intact, and free of defects before commencing any overhead work. This practice is to be reinforced at the next toolbox talk and made standard procedure across all active crews.
Injury Location — Body Diagram
Color Guide
Primary injury site

Swelling / bruising area
Referred pain area
Confirmed Injury

Left hand, index finger — struck the scissor lift guardrail following wire snap. Diagnosis: contusion without nail damage (ICD S60.022A). No fracture confirmed via X-ray.

Injury Photographs
Pre-treatment — palm side
Pre-treatment — back of hand
Post-wrap — protective covering applied
Medical Evaluation & Treatment Summary
Clinical Findings
01
X-ray was performed to assess for fractures or broken bones. The attending physician determined no fractures or broken bones were present — only swelling was identified at the left index finger.
02
Topical ointment prescribed for swelling (apply every 4 hours). Tylenol and ibuprofen prescribed every 4–6 hours for pain management.
Treatment & Recovery
03
Left index finger was wrapped and fitted with a protective plastic covering to support the healing process.
04
As of Tuesday, May 12, 2026 — worker medically cleared and authorized to return to work without restrictions per attending physician.
